description: Read the complete contents of a file as text (deprecated; use read_text_file instead).
Read the contents of multiple files simultaneously, returning each file's content keyed by its path.
Create a new file or completely overwrite an existing file with new text content.
Make line-based edits to a text file, returning a git-style diff of the changes made.
Create a new directory (including nested directories) or silently succeed if it already exists.
Get a recursive JSON tree view of all files and directories under a given path.
Move or rename a file or directory from a source path to a destination path.
Return the list of directories (and their subdirectories) that this server is permitted to access.
Read the complete text contents of a single file, with optional head/tail line limiting.
Read an image or audio file and return its base64-encoded data along with its MIME type.
Get a detailed listing of all files and directories at a given path, prefixed with [FILE] or [DIR].
Get a detailed listing of all files and directories at a given path, including their sizes.
Recursively search for files and directories matching a glob-style pattern under a given path.
Retrieve detailed metadata (size, type, timestamps, permissions) about a file or directory.
Check the status of an agent job and retrieve results when complete.
Create a browser session for code execution via CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol).
Destroy a browser session.
Map a website to discover all indexed URLs on the site.
Starts a crawl job on a website and extracts content from all pages.
List browser sessions, optionally filtered by status.
Scrape content from a single URL with advanced options.
Search the web and optionally extract content from search results.
Check the status of a crawl job.
Extract structured information from web pages using LLM capabilities.
Autonomous web research agent.

Create and/or submit or delete a review of a pull request.

Find GitHub users by username, real name, or other profile information.

Execute a raw SQL query against the database with optional parameter values.
List all user tables in the SQLite database, excluding system tables.
Get the schema information for a specific table including column details.
Insert a new record into a table with specified data.
Read records from a table with optional conditions, limit, and offset.
